q: A _______ is a circuit component that stores electrical energy.
The capacitor is similar to a _______ battery.
The capacitor was originally known as a _______.
A capacitor stores electrical energy in the form of a/an _______ field.
A capacitor consists of two _______ separated by a small distance.
A/An _______ is inserted between its conductors.
The insulating material between the plates of the capacitor is known as the _______.
Film capacitors use plastic film as the _______ medium.
The working voltages of the film capacitors are up to _______ V.
The tolerance of the film capacitors range from 10% to _______.
Ceramic capacitors use ceramic as the _______ material.
_______ capacitors are used in high-frequency circuits such as audio frequency (af) to radio frequency (rf).
The capacitance of the ceramic capacitor can be altered by altering the _______ of the ceramic disc.
Ceramic capacitors are mainly used for _______.
The capacitance values of _______ capacitors are stable with respect to the applied voltage, the frequency, and the temperature.
Electrolytic capacitors use the _______ layer as the dielectric material.
Electrolytic capacitors are available with working voltages of up to approximately _______.
Electrolytic capacitors are used for filtering and smoothing rectified _______.
Variable capacitors mostly use _______ as the dielectric medium.
The capacitance of the _______ capacitor can be mechanically adjusted.
The variable capacitor is used to set the _______ frequency in tuner devices.
Variable capacitors are mostly used in _______ circuits, especially for high frequency, high power, and low loss.
_______ capacitors are used in radio tuning applications, antenna tuning, and so on.
A/An _______ is also known as an ultracapacitor.
_______ bridges the gap between electrolytic capacitors and rechargeable batteries.
Supercapacitor typically stores 10 to 100 times more energy than _______ capacitors.
Smaller units _______ are used as power backup for static random-access memory.
The charge of the capacitor is _______ proportional to the potential difference.
In the Sl system, the unit of the capacitance is _______.
The SI equivalent unit of farad is _______.
The SI unit named in honour of Michael Faraday is the _______.
The physical quantity whose Sl units is named in honour of Michael Faraday is the _______.
In practice, typical devices have _______ ranging from microfarad (ÂµF) to picofarad (pF).
The SI unit of the electric capacity of a conductor is the _______.
A _______ capacitor is the simplest capacitor.
A parallel-plate capacitor consists of two _______ metal plates separated by a dielectric.
The ratio of the _______ to the surface area is surface charge density.
The charge per _______ surface area is known as surface charge density.
The SI unit of surface charge density is _______.
The ratio of the surface charge density of the plate to the permittivity of the medium between the plates is the _______ between the plates.
The ratio of the potential difference between plates to the distance between the plates is the _______ between the plates.
For a given insulating material, the capacitance of a parallel-plate capacitor is _______ proportional to the area of its plates.
The capacitance of a parallel-plate capacitor is _______ proportional to plate-separation.
The ratio of the permittivity of a medium to that of the vacuum is the _______ of the medium.
The dielectric constant is known as _______ permittivity of the insulating material.
The dielectric constant of a dielectric is the ratio of its permittivity to that of _______.
When the capacitor is charged, work has been done for the transfer of _______ between the plates.
The _______ the values of the resistance and the capacitance, the longer it takes to charge the capacitor.
The product of the resistance and the capacitance is called the _______ constant of a capacitor charging or discharging process.
The time constant of a capacitor charging or discharging process is measured in _______.
A single capacitor which can replace the other two or more capacitors is called the _______ capacitor.
The equivalent capacitance of the capacitors connected in parallel is the sum of the _______ of the individual capacitors.
When different capacitors are connected in _______ each capacitor has the same charge.
When different capacitors are connected in _______ they have different potential differences.
When the capacitors are connected in _______ the reciprocal of the equivalent capacitance is the sum of the reciprocals of their capacitances.
